purpose: Define how Sandra and Marie routes can influence each other without turning Marie into automatic suspicion, Sandra into replacement, or Player into an incoherent protagonist.
core_principles: Marie remains a living official relationship, not a guilt mechanic.
Sandra remains a returning complicity, not a replacement for Marie.
Player can feel divided without becoming cynical or manipulative by default.
Cross-route pressure should appear first through presence, timing, tone, and attention.
Suspicion must be earned progressively, not triggered automatically.
The player's choices should shape whether tension is softened, hidden, displaced, or amplified.

presence.description: How available Player feels in each route.
presence.early_signal: shorter, more delayed, or more automatic replies.

attention.description: What Player remembers, notices, or fails to notice.
attention.early_signal: he remembers Sandra details while missing Marie’s ordinary cues, or the reverse.

tone.description: How alive, playful, careful, or absent Player sounds.
tone.early_signal: Marie can feel a change before she knows why.

timing.description: When Player answers, pauses, switches conversations, or reopens contact.

self_image.description: Which version of himself Player feels with each woman.
self_image.early_signal: Sandra recalls a lighter or more curious Player; Marie anchors present-life Player.

allowed_cross_route_effects: Marie notices Player is a bit elsewhere without naming Sandra.
Player carries a softer or more alive tone from one route into another.
Sandra’s return makes Player more aware of what he misses or has neglected.
Marie’s presence can make Sandra’s ambiguity feel more dangerous.
A good Marie interaction can make Player more careful with Sandra.
A strong Sandra callback can make Player more attentive or more avoidant with Marie.
Cross-route pressure can appear through delays, tone changes, remembered details, or micro-repairs.
forbidden_cross_route_effects: Marie directly suspects Sandra without prior escalation.
Sandra directly attacks or belittles Marie.
Player automatically lies or manipulates as the default route behavior.
Marie becomes only an obstacle or punishment.
A route erases the memory or emotional value of the other route.
Cross-route pressure becomes melodrama too early.
player_consistency_rules: Player should remain discreet, observant, slightly ironic when embarrassed.
Player can be evasive, but not automatically cruel.
Player can minimize his trouble before understanding it.
Player should have recognizable habits across routes.
Player’s presence or absence must be readable through SMS behavior.
Choices must be actual SMS, not authorial moral labels.
marie_detection_rules: Marie detects quality of presence before facts.
Marie can notice when Player is "a little far away".
Marie should first respond through domestic humor, small checks, or soft requests for presence.
Marie should not name Sandra unless prior route escalation justifies it.
Marie’s pain should emerge from the present couple, not from abstract jealousy.
sandra_pressure_rules: Sandra creates pressure by returning, not by demanding.
Sandra can test whether Player also felt the distance.
Sandra can become more active without becoming forceful.
Sandra’s route can make Player feel more alive, but should not erase Marie’s value.

future_use.note: Future scene contracts can reference these rules when a scene involves route pressure, message switching, delayed replies, or emotional bleed between Sandra and Marie.
